channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.description: Google has accidentally leaked details about an unfixed issue in Chromium that keeps JavaScript running in the background even when the browser is closed, allowing remote code execution on the device....

item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: Fuel Tank Breaches Expand Scope of Iran's Cyber Offensive
item.description: Security experts have long warned that insecure automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems exposed on the Internet can be tampered with by threat actors....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Exchange 0-Day, npm Worm, Fake AI Repo, Cisco Exploit and More
item.description: Monday opens with a trust problem. A mail server flaw is under active use. A network control system was targeted. Trusted packages were poisoned. A fake model page pushed a stealer. Then came the fami...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Boring Stuff Is Dangerous Now
item.description: AI agents capable of discovering and exploiting obscure vulnerabilities are emerging alongside developers producing vast amounts of potentially flawed AI-generated code, forcing defenders to adapt acc...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: How to Reduce Phishing Exposure Before It Turns into Business Disruption
item.description: What happens when a phishing email looks clean enough to pass through security, but dangerous enough to expose the business after one click? That is the gap many SOCs still struggle with: the attacks ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Boulevard of Broken Dreams: 2 Decades of Cyber Fails
item.description: From the MGM and Caesars fiasco and MOVEit's patch nightmare to epic business blunders and the jaded reality of living in a post-breach world, Dark Reading looks back at the mistakes, miscalculations,...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Developer Workstations Are Now Part of the Software Supply Chain
item.description: Supply chain attackers are not only trying to slip malicious code into trusted software. They are trying to steal the access that makes trusted software possible. Recently, three separate campaigns hi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, n8n Patch RCE, SQL Injection, Privilege Escalation Flaws
item.description: Ivanti, Fortinet, n8n, SAP, and VMware have released security fixes for various vulnerabilities that could be exploited by bad actors to bypass authentication and execute arbitrary code. Topping the l...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: MiniPlasma Windows 0-Day Enables SYSTEM Privilege Escalation on Fully Patched Systems

item.title: Can Laws Stop Deepfakes? South Korea Aims to Find Out
item.description: South Korea's local elections next month will be a test bed for how effective regulations might be to stymie the flow of deepfakes....

item.title: NGINX CVE-2026-42945 Exploited in the Wild, Causing Worker Crashes and Possible RCE
item.description: A newly disclosed security flaw impacting NGINX Plus and NGINX Open has come under active exploitation in the wild, days after its public disclosure, according to VulnCheck. The vulnerability, tracked...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Grafana GitHub Token Breach Led to Codebase Download and Extortion Attempt
item.description:
Grafana has disclosed that an "unauthorized party" obtained a token that granted them the ability to access the company's GitHub environment and download its codebase.
"Our investigation has deter...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
